steps.prompt: Which word is the main finite verb?

hints.errare: 'errare' is an infinitive meaning 'to err' — it functions as the subject complement here
hints.insipientis: 'insipientis' is a noun/adjective meaning 'of a fool'
hints.perseverare: 'perseverare' is an infinitive meaning 'to persist' — not the finite verb

hints.edo, edere, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat) — 'est' here is from 'esse'
hints.eo, ire, ivi, itum: These are the parts of 'ire' (to go) — not 'esse'
hints.mitto, mittere, misi, missum: These are the parts of 'mittere' (to send) — unrelated

steps.target: to err / to make a mistake
choices: to err / to make a mistake

hints.to wander aimlessly: 'errare' can mean 'to wander,' but here Cicero uses it in the sense of 'to err / make mistakes'
hints.to plow: 'to plow' is 'arare' — similar sounding but different from 'errare'
hints.to blush: 'to blush' is 'erubescere' — do not confuse the er- prefix

steps.prompt: What tense and mood is 'errare' in this sentence?

hints.Present indicative: 'errare' is not a finite form — it is an infinitive functioning as the subject
hints.Imperfect subjunctive: Imperfect subjunctive would be 'erraret' — 'errare' is the infinitive
hints.Future infinitive: Future infinitive would be 'erraturum esse' — 'errare' is the present infinitive

hints.aro, arare, aravi, aratum: These are the parts of 'arare' (to plow) — not 'errare'
hints.erigo, erigere, erexi, erectum: These are the parts of 'erigere' (to raise up) — different er- verb
hints.erubesco, erubescere, erubui, —: These are the parts of 'erubescere' (to blush) — not 'errare'

steps.prompt: What tense and mood is 'perseverare' here?

hints.Present indicative: 'perseverare' is not a finite form — it is an infinitive complementing the implied 'est'
hints.Perfect infinitive: Perfect infinitive would be 'perseveravisse' — 'perseverare' is present
hints.Future infinitive: Future infinitive would be 'perseveraturum esse' — 'perseverare' is present

hints.servo, servare, servavi, servatum: These are the parts of 'servare' (to save) — not 'perseverare'
hints.persuadeo, persuadere, persuasi, persuas: These are the parts of 'persuadere' (to persuade) — different verb
hints.percipio, percipere, percepi, perceptum: These are the parts of 'percipere' (to perceive) — not 'perseverare'

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'homo' — 'hominis' is the genitive singular

hints.1st: 1st declension genitive ends in -ae — 'homo, hominis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension genitive ends in -i — 'hominis' with -is ending is 3rd declension
hints.5th: 5th declension genitive ends in -ei — 'hominis' is 3rd declension

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'insipiens' — 'insipientis' is the genitive singular
hints.Accusative: Accusative singular would be 'insipientem' — 'insipientis' is genitive
hints.Ablative: Ablative singular would be 'insipiente/insipientī' — 'insipientis' is genitive

hints.1st: 1st declension genitive ends in -ae — 'insipiens, insipientis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension genitive ends in -i — 'insipientis' is 3rd declension
hints.4th: 4th declension genitive ends in -us — 'insipientis' is 3rd declension

translation: It is the nature of any person to err, but of no one except a fool to persist in error.
